next_agent_suggestions: User runtime validation: boot the game, navigate to /play, confirm 4 chrome CRITICALs no longer fire. Smoke test by visiting / to confirm checks still trigger on chromed routes.
notes:
Pathname-detection mechanism: re-used the existing `state.route` field already populated at uxSafety.js:52 via `window.location.pathname`. captureUIState() already snapshots the pathname into state.route, so enforceUXRules() can read it directly without a fresh `window.location` lookup — this matches the existing intra-module pattern (state-flows-into-enforcement) and avoids divergence between the captured snapshot and a re-read at enforcement time. Note AppLayout.jsx itself uses react-router's `location.pathname.startsWith('/play')`, but the task explicitly required exact-match (no prefix) and uxSafety has no react-router context anyway, so window-pathname via state.route is the right primitive here.

Diff summary: 1 file (src/renderer/utils/uxSafety.js), +25 / -6 LOC (well under the 50-LOC cap). Single-file confirmed via `git diff --stat`. Changes: (1) added ROUTE_EXEMPTIONS module-scope export with comment block referencing AppLayout.jsx:27,42 and the 'ZERO app chrome' rationale; (2) initial entry exempts /play from MUST_HAVE_HOME_BUTTON, MUST_HAVE_HEADER, MUST_HAVE_NAV_CONTAINER, NO_EMPTY_SCREEN; (3) enforceUXRules() builds an exemptedRules array by exact-match lookup (`ROUTE_EXEMPTIONS[state.route] || []`) and gates each rule check via an `isExempt(rule)` predicate; (4) ROUTE_EXEMPTIONS added to the default export object for symmetry with UX_RULES.

Exact-match guarantee: lookup is `ROUTE_EXEMPTIONS[state.route]` — object key equality. `/play-back`, `/play/foo`, `/playback` all hash to different keys and return undefined → defaults to empty array → no rules exempted. Verified by reading the implementation in the final file.
